conjecture_title: Informal open question on the exact value of $b_\varepsilon$
paper_title: A note on sublinear separators and expansion
summary: Dvořák establishes the bounds $\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}-1\leq b_\varepsilon\leq\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}-\frac{1}{2}$ for $0<\varepsilon<\frac{1}{2}$ and asks whether the remaining gap of $\frac{1}{2}$ can be closed; no follow-up paper resolving this question was found in five web searches. The question of whether $b_\varepsilon$ is discontinuous at $\varepsilon=1/2$ remains open.
search_queries: Dvořák sublinear separators expansion b_epsilon bounds 2001.09679
sublinear separator expansion "b_epsilon" OR "b_varepsilon" graph theory bounds discontinuity
Dvorak "2001.09679" citation "sublinear separator" expansion bounds improved 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up work resolving the gap between the upper and lower bounds on $b_\varepsilon$ was found in the indexed literature. The paper is 6 years old (2020–2026), so medium rather than high confidence is assigned. Related work on strongly sublinear separators (e.g., arXiv:2208.10074 on product structure, and a SoCG 2025 paper on sphere intersection graphs) continues the broader programme but does not appear to address this specific bound-tightening question.
